plugin.name: theorems-harness
plugin.description: Theorem's Harness: GraphQL-first MCP for adaptive planning, context, coordination, execution, validation, memory, and learning.

interface.shortDescription: Adaptive session routing for grounded planning, context, coordination, execution, memory, and reporting.
interface.longDescription: Theorem's Harness is the plugin surface for grounded agent work. /harness is opt-in to adaptive harness behavior: the agent observes, chooses the right capability mix, coordinates when peers may overlap, executes bounded slices, validates claims, and preserves high-signal learning without forcing every task through one narrow mode.
defaultPrompt: Use Theorem's Harness for planning, implementation, debugging, review, validation, coordination, memory, and reports.
Treat /harness as opt-in routing, not a forced mode. Pick the best capabilities and keep moving unless blocked.
When available, use harness_route to inspect the recommended capability mix before or during a run.
Prefer Harness GraphQL tools when available; use legacy flat tools only for fallback or compatibility diagnosis.
For complex planning handoffs, use /harness mode=plan handoff=spark and keep planner review in-thread.
For multi-head work, announce in the shared room, state footprint, and resolve semantic-overlap tensions there.
Harness may delegate to observe, theorize, plan, execute, diagnose, coordinate, research, validate, and review.
Use stable checklist IDs when they reduce risk; do not claim done unless validation supports it.
For UI projects, track Runtime, Product, and Vision completion; compare screenshots and run Do Not Downgrade gates.
For Redis, THG product, RESP/Valkey, TheoremContextClient, or HotGraph changes, consult the relevant specialist.
When AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md opts in, prefer harness prepare/preview/run for multi-file or risky work; skip trivial routing.
For cross-head work, read room context, heartbeat, write coordination_intent, consume mentions, and save durable decisions.
Keep CRDT/Rust runtime changes in Theorem/RustyRed repos; this plugin ships prompts, hooks, skills, and proxy wiring.
Treat semantic-overlap tensions as awareness signals, not locks; resolve them in the room and keep writes nonblocking.
Before commit, PR, or launch-ready reports after multi-agent work, run peer-review and reconcile findings.
Treat plugin-qualified Harness tool labels as routing names; use product language unless wire IDs matter.
Use encode for high-signal feedback, solutions, and postmortems; keep secrets out of stored memory.

Apply the source-attributed practice graph for specification, planning, test-first execution, debugging, review, and evidence-before-completion; use subagents when the host exposes a suitable model and the work decomposes cleanly.
